Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Electron beam powder bed fusion of Y₂O₃/\gamma-TiAl nanocomposite with balanced strength and toughness

Composition_Info.Note: Pre-alloyed Ti-48Al-2Cr-2Nb powders (45-150 um) mixed with 0.4 wt.% Y2O3 nanoparticles (~30 nm). Y2O3 appears as both rod-like (monoclinic) and spherical (cubic) forms in the as-built sample.

Process_Text.original: PBF-EB processing on Arcam A2XX machine using multi-spot melt strategy. Build temperature 1160 degC, vacuum 1x10^-2 mbar controlled by helium regulating gas. Layer thickness 70 um. Four concurrent beam spots utilized. Parameters: scanning speed 600 mm/s, beam current 25 mA, line offset 0.5 mm, focus offset 0 mA, line energy density 2.5 J/mm, spot number 50, spot time 0.8 ms, minimum distance per spot 4 mm. 90 deg scan rotation between layers.
Process_Text.simplified: EBM multi-spot melt strategy, layer thickness 70 um (L70), build temp 1160 degC, line energy density 2.5 J/mm.

Microstructure_Text.original: Duplex microstructure with alpha2/gamma lamellar colonies, equiaxed gamma grains and B2 grains. Phase constitution: 49.7% alpha2-phase, 40.2% gamma-phase, 10.1% B2-phase. Lamellar spacing 208 +/- 72 nm with straight alpha2/gamma interface. Y2O3 nanoparticles uniformly distributed (90 +/- 38 nm, 0.28 +/- 0.14 um^-2, 0.8 +/- 0.2 vol%). Twins and dislocations present in equiaxed gamma grains and within gamma lamellae. Lamellar colony fraction 54-61% with colony sizes 33-42 um measured at different locations.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Duplex microstructure (alpha2/gamma lamellar colonies + equiaxed gamma + B2 grains). Finer lamellar spacing 208 nm, lower gamma/B2 phase fractions, uniformly dispersed ~90 nm Y2O3 nanoparticles.

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.EBSD: Phase constitution and orientation mapping with 0.1-0.5 um step sizes
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.HRTEM: Interface coherency analysis between Y2O3 and TiAl
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.TEM: Lamellar structure, SADP, FFT, IFFT analysis

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Strain rate 2x10^-4 s^-1, ISO 783-1999, Epsilon extensometer gauge length 25 mm

Properties_Info.Note: Ductile material behavior. Slightly lower than L90 (18.4%) attributed to lower gamma-phase fraction.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: SENB specimens per ASTM E399, UTM4204 with clip-on gauge
Properties_Info.Note: Significantly higher than L90 (11.7 MPa sqrt(m)) and exceeds published K_IC for PBF-EB gamma-TiAl

Composition_Info.Note: Pre-alloyed Ti-48Al-2Cr-2Nb powders (45-150 um) mixed with 0.4 wt.% Y2O3 nanoparticles (~30 nm).

Process_Text.original: PBF-EB processing on Arcam A2XX machine using multi-spot melt strategy. Build temperature 1160 degC, vacuum 1x10^-2 mbar controlled by helium regulating gas. Layer thickness 90 um. Four concurrent beam spots utilized. Parameters: scanning speed 600 mm/s, beam current 25 mA, line offset 0.5 mm, focus offset 0 mA, line energy density 2.5 J/mm, spot number 50, spot time 0.8 ms, minimum distance per spot 4 mm.
Process_Text.simplified: EBM multi-spot melt strategy, layer thickness 90 um (L90), build temp 1160 degC, line energy density 2.5 J/mm.

Microstructure_Text.original: Duplex microstructure with alpha2/gamma lamellar colonies, equiaxed gamma grains and B2 grains. Top layer fully lamellar, bulk shows duplex structure. Phase constitution differs from L70: gamma-phase fraction ~75%, alpha2-phase 50% in bulk. Lamellar degradation mechanisms: alpha2 lamellae decomposition with gamma merging via alpha2->gamma, and gamma/B2 grain formation at colony boundaries.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Duplex microstructure with higher gamma-phase fraction (75%) compared to L70. Top layer fully lamellar, bulk shows degradation via alpha2 decomposition and colony boundary spheroidisation.

Properties_Info.Note: Higher elongation than L70 due to higher gamma-phase fraction

Composition_Info.Note: Pre-alloyed Ti-48Al-2Cr-2Nb powders (45-150 um) mixed with 0.4 wt.% Y2O3 nanoparticles (~30 nm). This sample has lack-of-fusion defects due to Y2O3 addition impacting melting behavior.

Process_Text.original: PBF-EB processing on Arcam A2XX machine using hatch melt (reference condition). Build temperature 1160 degC, vacuum 1x10^-2 mbar. Layer thickness 70 um. Parameters: scanning speed 2400 mm/s, beam current 8 mA, line offset 0.1 mm, focus offset 0 mA, line energy density 0.2 J/mm. Three outlines with 0.5 mm offset for contour melt, electron beam meandered back-and-forth during hatch melt with 90 deg scan rotation between layers.
Process_Text.simplified: EBM hatch melt strategy (reference), layer thickness 70 um, build temp 1160 degC, line energy density 0.2 J/mm.

Microstructure_Text.original: Duplex microstructure with higher gamma-phase fraction (70.4%) compared to multi-spot samples. Phase constitution: 14.3% alpha2, 70.4% gamma, 15.3% B2. Lamellar spacing coarser at 959 +/- 155 nm. Nanoparticle size larger at 218 +/- 113 nm with some chain-like arrangement. Number density 0.09 +/- 0.03 um^-2, volume fraction 0.9 +/- 0.4%. Dislocations blocked by twins and grain boundaries. Parallel dislocation arrangement suggesting twin formation via dislocation pile-up. Presence of dislocation sub-grain boundaries indicating recovery and recrystallisation. Lack-of-fusion defects present with defect area fraction 1.6%.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Duplex microstructure with higher gamma-phase (70.4%), coarser lamellar spacing (959 nm), larger nanoparticles (218 nm) with chain-like distribution. 1.6% defect area fraction from lack-of-fusion.

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.TEM: Lamellar structure, twins and dislocations observation
